description: In Zulu, high lexical tones frequently spread allophonically to following low-tone syllables, raising their pitch to a level just below that of adjacent high-tone syllables. This is an intonational process affecting tone realization rather than lexical contrast.
sources: Doke, C. M. (1931). _An Outline of Zulu Grammar_. Johannesburg: Witwatersrand University Press.
Maddieson, I. (2013). _The Sounds of the World's Languages_ (2nd ed.). Oxford: Wiley-Blackwell.
Ziervogel, D. M. (1963). _A Concise Grammar of Zulu_. Pietermaritzburg: Shuter and Shooter.
